summary.intent: Fix the Gossamer Electron app's loading spinner issue, ensure local git checkpoints are indexed reliably, and improve the checkpoint detail UI with better formatting, markdown rendering, and a proper diff viewer.
summary.outcome: Fixed async git fetch blocking the event loop, stabilized the poll loop to use local branch refs, restored missing npm scripts, added a diff2html-based file diff viewer with full dark/light mode theming, and applied InlineMarkdown rendering to checkpoint summary text fields.

repo: Gossamer indexes `entire/checkpoints/v1` via a git worktree at `.gossamer/checkpoints`; the poll loop must use `refs/heads/` prefix to avoid git resolving the branch name to the remote tracking ref when both point to the same SHA.

The `entire` post-commit hook (`entire hooks git post-commit`) creates checkpoint commits on `entire/checkpoints/v1` and pushes to origin; if the push fails, it rolls back the local branch with `git reset --hard origin/...`, so checkpoints disappear locally.
Shadow/in-progress sessions live on `entire/<hash>-<hash>` branches and only appear on `entire/checkpoints/v1` after the post-commit hook fires successfully.
The app uses Mantine's `light-dark(lightVal, darkVal)` CSS function (not `@media prefers-color-scheme`) for theming — third-party CSS must follow the same pattern to work correctly.
`CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` has `gitOid` as `@id` and `checkpointId` as a plain index, so lookups by `checkpointId` must use `findFirst`, not `findUnique`.
The `MarkdownView.tsx` component already exports `InlineMarkdown` for inline rendering of bold, italic, and backtick code — it was already imported in SessionDetail.tsx via ClaudeTurnCard.

code.finding: Original `execSync('git fetch origin')` had no timeout and blocked the Node.js event loop, freezing all HTTP responses and causing the UI spinner to hang indefinitely.
Duplicate `GIT_LOCAL_ENV` const declarations in the same block scope were introduced during SSH suppression attempts and had to be cleaned up.

`merge --ff-only` in the poll loop was inadvertently fast-forwarding the local branch to match origin, including bad commits accidentally pushed during debugging. Removed entirely — gossamer should only reset the worktree to the local branch tip, never advance it.
Files changed section was hidden when `fileCount === 0 && diffStats === null`; the condition needed to show the section unconditionally when expanded (showing 'Loading…' while fetch is in-flight).

diff2html's `colorScheme: 'auto'` uses `@media prefers-color-scheme` which conflicts with Mantine's data-attribute scheme. Must pass `colorScheme: 'light'` and use `light-dark()` CSS function for all overrides.

workflow: Setting `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0` and `BatchMode=yes` only suppresses terminal prompts — macOS Keychain still shows a GUI SSH dialog that bypasses both. The right fix is ensuring ssh-agent is properly configured, not suppressing prompts in code.

Testing git push behavior by making an `--allow-empty` commit in a worktree can accidentally stage and push pending deletions if the worktree has unstaged changes — always check `git status` in the worktree first.

diff2html CSS custom properties (`--d2h-*`) don't override cleanly when the library's own `:root` declarations have equal specificity; direct property overrides with `!important` or `light-dark()` are more reliable.
`concurrently` with `--names` and `-c` flags gives color-coded labeled output when running multiple dev processes (API server + Electron app) from a single `npm start`.

friction: Multiple rounds of SSH suppression attempts (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT, BatchMode, SSH_AUTH_SOCK, --no-track) before user decided to revert all of it and rely on the user's ssh-agent being configured correctly.
Accidentally pushed a bad commit to origin while testing push behavior — a test `git commit --allow-empty` in the worktree staged pending deletions and pushed them, requiring a force-push to recover.
diff2html dark mode theming required three rewrites (CSS custom properties → !important media query → light-dark()) before landing on the correct pattern matching the rest of the app.

The `entire` post-commit hook suppresses all errors with `2>/dev/null || true`, making silent failures invisible and hard to diagnose — required manually running the hook with stderr visible to find the issue.

Git ref ambiguity: bare branch name `entire/checkpoints/v1` resolved to `origin/entire/checkpoints/v1` when both pointed to the same SHA, causing the worktree reset to anchor to origin instead of local.
open_items: `filesTouched` is empty in the DB for checkpoint `d2014fd45299` and possibly others — need to investigate whether the session/root `metadata.json` files contain `files_touched` and whether the indexer reads it correctly.
The `entire` post-commit hook silently swallows all errors; worth removing `2>/dev/null || true` or at least logging failures so broken hook runs are detectable.

Checkpoint `18f992950c10` referenced in the `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer of commit `35f7367` was never written to `entire/checkpoints/v1` (hook misfired) and cannot be retroactively recreated — that checkpoint data is permanently lost.

The diff viewer falls back to a plain `filesTouched` filename list when no `CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` mapping exists for a checkpoint — checkpoints without a git OID mapping will never show actual diffs.
